data.title: Lumifi Named to Inc.’s 2026 List of the Fastest-Growing Private Companies in the Southwest
data.description: In Scottsdale, Arizona, Lumifi has secured the 25th spot on the Inc. Regionals: Southwest list by Inc., a prominent media brand for entrepreneurs and business executives. This prestigious ranking acknowledges the fastest-growing privately held companies in Arizona, New Mexico, Oklahoma, and Texas. The Inc. Regionals list serves as an extension of the renowned national Inc. 5000 list and showcases independent small businesses that are pivotal in propelling economic advancement throughout the Southwest region. The companies featured on this year's list have demonstrated remarkable growth and potential.

data.title: The Ultimate Knowledge Institute (UKi) Appoints Shawn Turskey and Mike Salfity as Independent Board Directors
data.description: In Scottsdale, Arizona, the Ultimate Knowledge Institute (UKi), a top provider of cybersecurity workforce readiness solutions, has appointed Shawn Turskey and Mike Salfity as Independent Directors to its Board of Directors. This announcement aligns with UKi's focus on advancing its AI-driven cybersecurity training and workforce readiness strategies. The inclusion of Shawn and Mike enhances the board's expertise, marking a significant step for UKi in strengthening its leadership.

data.title: Pathways LA to Host "Seeds of Hope 2026: Havana Nights" on May 16 at The Millwick
data.description: On April 1, 2026, Pathways LA is set to organize Seeds of Hope 2025: Havana Nights, their prominent fundraising event. The event will take place on May 16 from 5:00 to 9:00 p.m. at The Millwick in downtown Los Angeles. The event will be hosted by Wendy Burch from KTLA 5.

data.title: New post-quantum blockchain by Naoris introduces quantum-resistant cryptography; Bitcoin, Ethereum devs respond to threat.
data.description: The Naoris Protocol has implemented quantum-resistant cryptography in its blockchain network to safeguard against potential future threats, aligning with the broader crypto industry. This advanced security measure is crucial in protecting data and assets, especially given the increasing prominence of quantum technology. By incorporating this cutting-edge technology, Naoris aims to enhance the security and resilience of its network while staying ahead of potential risks.

data.title: UBCF Offers Free Shopping for Breast Cancer Patients & Survivors April 14-25
data.description: In a bid to support breast cancer patients and survivors dealing with financial difficulties post-diagnosis, the United Breast Cancer Foundation (UBCF) has been organizing monthly shopping events in Huntington Station, New York. The purpose behind these events is to alleviate the financial strains and stress experienced by these individuals. The events are free of charge and aim to aid those affected by breast cancer. Scheduled regularly, these events align with UBCF's objective of providing assistance to those in need.

data.title: GoPro Cameras Onboard Historic Artemis II Mission to the Moon
data.description: In San Mateo, California, on April 2, 2026, GoPro, Inc. revealed that its cameras, commonly listed as GPRO on NASDAQ, are actively documenting the Artemis II mission. These cameras are onboard to record footage of the Orion spacecraft during its journey around the Moon and return to Earth. Artemis II represents NASA's maiden crewed expedition, signaling a significant milestone in space exploration.

data.title: Afterburner CEO Christian "Boo" Boucousis Announces New Book Flawless Leadership as Firm Marks 30 Years in Business
data.description: Celebrating its 30th anniversary, Afterburner, based in Miami, is set to release a new book titled "Flawless Leadership: The Perfect Book for The Imperfect Leader" by CEO Christian "Boo" Boucousis. The book focuses on leadership, artificial intelligence (AI), and decision-making.

data.title: Coca-Cola Beverages Florida Unveils America250 Commemorative Cans
data.description: In Jacksonville, Florida, Coca-Cola Beverages Florida, LLC revealed special America250 Coca-Cola cans at their manufacturing site, one of the few in the U.S. creating packaging for all 50 states to celebrate the upcoming 250th anniversary of the nation. "Coca-Cola, a part of American culture for almost 140 years, holds a special place in our history," stated Troy Taylor, Chairman & CEO of Coke Florida.

data.title: Bluerock Private Real Estate Fund Announces Monthly Distribution for April 2026
data.description: On April 2, 2026, the Bluerock Private Real Estate Fund (BPRE) disclosed its upcoming monthly distribution for April 2026. Shareholders as of April 15, 2026, will receive a cash distribution of $0.1167 per share on April 30, 2026.

data.title: Precast Concrete Expert Scott Stavig Explains Vaults, Grease Interceptors, and Oil-Water Separators in HelloNation
data.description: In Medford, Oregon on April 2, 2026, a report by HelloNation suggests that expert Scott Stavig recommends utilizing precast vaults, grease interceptors, and oil-water separation systems as effective methods for managing fluids and safeguarding underground utilities during construction projects. These systems are crucial for ensuring the efficient flow and protection of substances beneath the surface.

data.title: Two New Richmond American Communities Debuting in Victorville
data.description: Monarch & Sierra at Sky Trail in VICTORVILLE, California is currently displaying a diverse selection of new homes. The showcase is led by Richmond American Homes of Maryland, Inc., a subsidiary of Sekisui, holding California Department of Real Estate's Real Estate Broker Corporation License Number 01842595. This event, held on April 2, 2026, highlights a range of new properties for potential buyers.

data.title: CUPE Alberta raises alarm at Provincial Government’s latest Municipal takeover attempt
data.description: In Edmonton, AB, the recently introduced Bill 28, known as the Municipal Affairs and Housing Statutes Amendment Act, is seen as an effort by the UCP to assert more influence over municipalities and essential services in Alberta. Raj Uppal, President of CUPE Alberta, criticized the bill, pointing out the irony of a provincial government criticizing federal overreach while seeking to diminish municipal autonomy. Bill 28 grants the UCP government the authority to mandate a municipality to relinquish ownership or control over certain aspects of public services. This move has raised concerns about potential implications for local governance and services.
